# CANDIDATE: BEAR_SCORE_6_RELAXATION

**Filed:** 2026-06-21
**Filer:** chef-nemotron (free-tier autonomous R&D)
**Type:** filter_change
**Status:** DRAFT (NEEDS-RATIFICATION per Rule 9)

## Hypothesis

Relaxing the bear_score threshold from 8 to 6 (i.e., requiring bear_score>=6 instead of >=8) will increase the number of qualifying trades, capturing more of the J winner days (especially 4/29 and 5/01 which were lost under bear_score>=7) while still avoiding excessive losses on loser days. We expect that the additional trades on winner days will outweigh the additional losses on loser days, resulting in a net increase in edge_capture above the 771 threshold.

## Mechanism

The bear_score is a composite measure of bearish setup quality (components include level rejection, EMA ribbon flip, confluence, etc.). The current engine requires bear_score>=8 to trigger a BEARISH_REJECTION_RIDE_THE_RIBBON entry. We propose lowering the threshold to bear_score>=6. Entry and exit logic remain unchanged: chart stop primary, chandelier profit-lock, premium stop catastrophe cap, etc.

## OP-20 disclosures

1. **Account-size assumption:** qty=28 requires $25K+; $1K paper ~= 14% headline
2. **Sample bias:** We propose to test on the full 16-month SPY 5m dataset (Jan 2025 to Jun 2026). Selection method: all days where the engine would trigger under the relaxed filter. Overfit risk: relaxing a filter may increase noise; we will monitor walk-forward stability and concentration.
3. **Out-of-sample:** NEEDS-OOS (we will perform a walk-forward held-out window after Stage-1)
4. **Real-fills:** NEEDS-REAL-FILLS (we will check top 3 J days with realistic OPRA simulation)
5. **Failure modes:** Worst day: potential large loss on a loser day if the filter admits too many low-quality trades; max drawdown: could increase if we take more losing trades; blow-up scenario: if the relaxed filter leads to taking trades on strong bull days (though we are bear-only, so less likely).
6. **Concentration:** We will compute after Stage-1; if top-5 days exceed 50% of P&L, we will flag.

## Pre-merge gate

Gym validators: 67/67 PASS (no regression in existing logic); Walk-forward: OOS/IS Sharpe ratio >= 0.70 on 16-month data with a 6-month OOS window; Real-fills: top 3 J days must show <=20% diff between BS-sim and realistic OPRA fills; Additionally, we must verify that the relaxed filter does not cause the engine to take trades on bull days (via VIX or HTF checks) but note we are only relaxing a bear_score threshold, so the engine still only takes bear trades.

## Confidence

4 / 10 -- The BEAR_SCORE_7_RELAXATION showed that a similar relaxation (to 7) was dependent on the 5/04 outlier. We hope that 6 might capture 4/29 and 5/01 without being too noisy, but there is significant risk of overfit.

## Pre-existing leaderboard impact

This candidate complements the BEAR_SCORE_7_RELAXATION (which was rejected) by trying a different threshold. It does not conflict with any existing leaderboard candidates that are watcher-only or new trade classes. It may conflict with any other bear_score threshold relaxations, but none are currently on the leaderboard.
